candidates.row_label: Amortization in Cost of Goods and Services
candidates.why: This is a D&A component within the COGS area, but the selected parent row 'Cost of sales' already appears to include it. In the visible years, 'Cost of Goods and Services' plus this row and 'Depreciation in Cost of Goods and Services' reconciles to 'Cost of sales', so adding it separately would double count.

candidates.row_label: Depreciation in Cost of Goods and Services
candidates.why: This is a D&A component within the COGS area, but the selected parent row 'Cost of sales' already appears to include it. The line-item bridge indicates it rolls up into 'Cost of sales', so it should not be added separately.

variables.notes: Selected COGS reference bucket is [Income Statement] Cost of sales. The statement structure shows the COGS D&A rows are sub-components embedded in that selected total, not separate same-level expenses to add later.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Intangibles in Selling Expenses
candidates.why: This is a D&A component within selling expenses, but the selected parent row 'Selling and marketing expenses' already appears to include it. The nearby balancing row indicates the selected selling expense line is the total including amortization.
This would belong to the SG&A bucket if populated, but it does not show usable values in the excerpt and there is no evidence it sits outside the selected selling expense total.

This is a D&A component within administrative expenses, but the selected parent row 'General and administrative expenses' already appears to include it. The lower 'Administrative Expenses' row plus amortization and depreciation reconciles to the selected G&A total.

This is a D&A component within administrative expenses, but the selected parent row 'General and administrative expenses' already appears to include it based on the reconciliation to the lower 'Administrative Expenses' row.

variables.notes: Selected XSGA reference bucket is the sum of [Income Statement] Selling and marketing expenses and [Income Statement] General and administrative expenses. The visible D&A rows for selling and admin behave as sub-components of those selected totals, so adding them separately would double count. No separate above-EBIT R&D expense row is selected, and there is no explicit 'Amortization in R&D' row to consider.
